question.text: In option terminology, what does 'exercise' mean?

answer.text: The act of the option buyer putting the right to buy or sell into effect
The act of the option seller cancelling the contract
The daily mark-to-market settlement of option positions
The process of rolling over an option to a later expiry date

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Exercise means the option buyer decides to use their right -- to buy (call) or sell (put) the underlying at the strike price. **The Trap:** Only the buyer can exercise. The seller cannot cancel. Mark-to-market applies to futures. Rolling over is a separate trading action. **Cross-Exam Context:** Exercise terminology is fundamental across all NISM derivative certifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For European options (like exchange-traded IROs in India), exercise happens only at expiry. For American options, the buyer can choose to exercise at any time.
